error.signature: ROS2 parameter change callback not firing in composed (component) nodes

verdict.summary: Parameter callbacks in composed nodes can silently fail when multiple component nodes register callbacks. The parameter event publisher topic is shared, and callback ordering depends on composition order. Also, add_on_set_parameters_callback only fires for set_parameter, not declare_parameter.

dead_ends.action: Register parameter callback and expect it to fire on declare_parameter
dead_ends.why_fails: add_on_set_parameters_callback only triggers on set_parameter/set_parameters calls, NOT on declare_parameter with initial value.

dead_ends.action: Use a single parameter callback for all parameters in a composed container
dead_ends.why_fails: Each component node has its own parameter namespace. Callbacks are per-node, not per-container. But parameter events from other nodes in the composition can interfere.

workarounds.action: Use add_on_set_parameters_callback and set values explicitly after declaration
workarounds.how: self.declare_parameter('speed', 1.0); self.add_on_set_parameters_callback(self.param_cb); # callback fires on future set_parameter calls

workarounds.action: Use parameter event subscribers for cross-node parameter monitoring
workarounds.how: from rcl_interfaces.msg import ParameterEvent; self.create_subscription(ParameterEvent, '/parameter_events', self.param_event_cb, 10)

workarounds.action: Separate parameter namespaces explicitly in composed nodes
workarounds.how: Use node name as namespace: Node('my_node', namespace='robot1', parameter_overrides=[...])
